name: Deviled Halibut
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Vegetable Oil or Olive Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large glass or stainless steel bowl, beat the oil and finely chopped garlic together with a whisk until thoroughly mixed. Add a few grindings of pepper and mix well.
2. Add the halibut fillet pieces to the marinade and turn until coated. Marinate at least 2 hours, up to 24 hours.
3. In a small saucepan, combine the tomato puree, finely chopped garlic, curry powder, lemon juice, soy sauce, and chicken stock. Heat to boiling, stirring thoroughly, then hold at a simmer.
4. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Combine the bread crumbs and grated cheddar cheese in a plastic bag. Coat the halibut pieces a few at a time, then place them on a non-fat cooking sprayed flat dish. Arrange the fish side by side in one layer only.
6. Bake the fish undisturbed in the oven for about 10 minutes.
7. Remove the saucepan from heat and spoon about half the sauce evenly over the fish. Return the pan to the heat.
8. Continue baking the fish for about 5 more minutes, then serve immediately on the dish it was baked in. Present the remaining sauce in a separate dish.
Note: The flavor can vary with different curry powders or curry pastes. Chili enthusiasts may prefer Thai curries (great and very hot). Cheese variety can also be changed for different flavors; aged sharp cheddar is a family favorite.
